goal: Verify standalone staticcheck adds nothing; fix one ST1020 godoc-comment-format nit

summary.title: Standalone staticcheck.dev not needed; one-line ST1020 fix
summary.what: User asked whether staticcheck.dev (standalone Staticcheck) should be added alongside golangci-lint. Verified: golangci-lint vendors the same engine and we already enable it; running standalone with default checks returns zero findings. With -checks "all" the standalone tool finds 43 × ST1000 (package-comment style) + 1 × ST1020 (godoc form) — all style, no bugs. Fixed the ST1020 inline; everything else is opinionated style we deliberately do not gate on.
summary.why: Closing the question for the record: a separate Staticcheck install would add nothing that the existing golangci-lint config does not already catch. The one-line ST1020 fix verifies the conclusion (the issue is real, just style-tier). No separate CI integration is necessary.
summary.user_goal: Settle the staticcheck.dev question with evidence rather than assertion.

decisions.point: Add separate staticcheck CI step vs rely on golangci-lint
decisions.rationale: Same engine. Two CI steps running the same checks would cost CI minutes and diverge over time as the standalone and bundled versions update on different cadences.
43 callsites of pure documentation nit; would be noise. If we want package-doc enforcement that is a curated style PR with a real motivation, not a side-effect of a tooling question.

rejected.alternative: Enable the ST* category in golangci-lint config
rejected.reason: Style noise; would generate dozens of comment-form findings that are not bug-finder signal.

rejected.alternative: Add staticcheck as a separate make target / CI job
rejected.reason: Same engine as golangci-lint; would be redundant work that drifts.

followups: If the project ever wants enforced package-level documentation, open a focused PR enabling ST1000 + writing the 40+ package comments. Out of scope for the lint-tooling question.

architectural_claims: golangci-lint with staticcheck enabled is the only static-analysis tool the project requires; standalone Staticcheck is the same engine and adds no bug-finder signal.
ST* (style) checks remain off-by-default in this project; bug-finder linters are the gate.
